title: Dictionaries
overview: Dictionaries store data as key-value pairs — like a real dictionary where you look up a word (key) to find its definition (value).

blocks.title: What is a Dictionary?
blocks.body: A **dictionary** stores pairs of keys and values. Each key is unique and maps to a value.
bullets: Keys must be unique and immutable (strings, numbers)
Values can be anything: numbers, strings, lists, even other dicts
Access: student["name"] gets the value for key "name"
Dictionaries are unordered — accessed by key, not position

blocks.title: A Dictionary is Like a Phone Book
blocks.text: You look up a person's name (the key) to find their phone number (the value). Each name is unique. You search by name directly — that's how Python dictionaries work.

blocks.title: Create Your Profile
blocks.instructions: Create a dictionary with keys: name, age, city, hobby. Print each value.
blocks.hint: Use profile["key_name"] to access each value.
blocks.expectedOutput:
Name: Lakshanya
Age: 20
City: Chennai
Hobby: coding

blocks.title: Adding, Updating, and Deleting
blocks.body: Dictionaries are mutable — you can change them after creation.
bullets: Add: student["grade"] = "A" — creates a new key-value pair
Update: student["age"] = 21 — changes the existing value
Delete: del student["grade"] — removes the key-value pair
Check: "name" in student — returns True if key exists

blocks.title: Dictionary Modifications
blocks.code:
student = {"name": "Lakshanya", "age": 20}
print(student)

student["grade"] = "A"
print(student)

student["age"] = 21
print(student)

print("name" in student)
del student["grade"]
print(student)

blocks.title: Dictionary Methods
blocks.body: Dictionaries have useful methods for working with keys, values, and items.
bullets: .get(key) — safely gets value or returns None if missing

blocks.title: Dictionary Methods
blocks.expectedOutput:
Keys: dict_keys(['name', 'age', 'city'])
name: Lakshanya
age: 20
city: Chennai
None
N/A

blocks.title: Student Database
blocks.instructions: Create a student dict with name and 3 subject marks. Calculate average and add as new key. Print all items.
blocks.hint: Calculate (math + science + english) // 3. Add with student['average'] = avg.
blocks.expectedOutput:
name: Lakshanya
math: 85
science: 92
english: 78
average: 85

keyTakeaways.text: Dictionaries store key-value pairs with {key: value}
Keys must be unique and immutable
Add/update with dict[key] = value; delete with del

whatYouLearned: How to create dictionaries with key-value pairs
How to access values using keys
How to add, update, and delete dictionary entries
How to check if a key exists
